item.title: Fed’s Barr Warns of Risks Tied to Looser Wall Street Bank Rules
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr criticized moves from regulators over the past year to relax the rules for US lenders, saying the proposals “considerably weaken bank regulation and supervision.”

item.title: Elon Musk to Attend ASML’s Private Tech Event to Discuss Terafab
item.description: Elon Musk will virtually attend a closed door technology conference run by ASML Holding NV to discuss his Terafab project, which the chip-equipment maker considers a “serious endeavor.”

item.title: Frasers Weighs £500 Million Bid for Shopping Center, Sky Reports
item.description: Mike Ashley’s Frasers Group Plc is considering a £500 million ($667 million) bid for Metrocentre, one of the UK’s biggest shopping centers, Sky News reports.

item.title: Macro Soothsayers, Stock Analysts See Vastly Different Markets
item.description: The strongest earnings season in years is coming to a close, and the Wall Street analysts who focus on individual companies have rarely been this bullish.

item.title: Asia-to-US Container Rates Spike 109% Since Iran War Started
item.description: Container shipping rates jumped over the past week amid higher fuel costs, congestion at some Asian ports and a pickup in demand heading into a peak season for booking ocean freight.

item.title: Publishers Adapt to AI, Space Race Centers on Satellites
item.description: People Inc. finds ways around the disruption.

item.title: Paramount Open to Selling Kids Channels to Quell EU Fears Over $110 Billion Warner Deal
item.description: Paramount Skydance Corp. is prepared — if necessary — to divest some children’s TV network assets to help win European Union approval of its $110 billion bid for Warner Bros. Discovery Inc.

item.title: Big Oil Is Changing Its Mind About Venezuela
item.description: No longer reluctant, industry executives are on the ground looking for deals. The Iran war has helped.

item.title: Charting the Global Economy: Jobs, Inflation Feed Rate-Hike Bets
item.description: A blowout US jobs report prompted bets that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates in 2026 as the ongoing Iran war fans inflation risk.

item.title: China’s Regulator to Deepen Focus on Returns in Fund Overhaul
item.description: The securities regulator in China signaled that the fund management industry needs to accelerate its shift toward delivering sustainable long-term returns.

item.title: Why Susquehanna Is Building a Prediction Markets Business
item.description: Creating an asset class for bets on anything.

item.title: Odd Lots: Why Susquehanna Builds Prediction Markets (Podcast)
item.description: Prediction markets that enable you to bet on pretty much everything are everywhere nowadays. But there’s still a big question over whether they can expand to include larger institutional investors like hedge funds. Part of the problem is that a lot of prediction market contracts are illiquid and trading volumes can sometimes be shallow. That’s where trading firm Susquehanna International Group comes in. In this episode, recorded live at New York’s City Winery, we talk to Jeremy Maletz, Susquehan

item.title: Iceland Debates Benefits of Euro Entry as Vote on EU Talks Nears
item.description: Iceland’s upcoming referendum on talks with the European Union has also sparked a debate over whether the country should give up its currency.

item.title: Jensen Huang Commands Seoul Stage With Night of Street Theatre
item.description: For one Friday night, Hongdae wasn’t Seoul’s trendiest neighborhood. It was Jensen Huang’s stage.

item.title: RBNZ’s Breman Says Country Finding Solutions to Protectionism
item.description: New Zealand’s central bank governor, Anna Breman, is seeing encouraging signs that will help counter the negative effects of deepening protectionism and a decline in global cooperation.

item.title: Austria Downgrade Ends Era in Club of Europe’s Safest Borrowers
item.description: Austria lost its last top credit score from a major assessor because of persistently high budget deficits, ending a decades-long streak as one of Europe’s best-rated issuers.

item.title: Philippine Court Drops Remaining Marcos Ill-Gotten Wealth Case
item.description: The Philippine anti-graft court dismissed the remaining claims in a civil case seeking to recover assets allegedly acquired by the late former President Ferdinand Marcos and his family.

item.title: China, New Zealand Hold Trade Talks to Deepen Cooperation
item.description: China and New Zealand held a bilateral trade commission meeting in Beijing on Friday, where officials exchanged views on deepening economic ties and cooperation in regional and multilateral frameworks.

item.title: North Korea’s Kim Says He Sees Nuclear Deterrent Role for Navy
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un said the navy must become part of the country’s nuclear war deterrent, using a ship inspection to underscore Pyongyang’s military ambitions ahead of Chinese President Xi Jinping’s expected visit next week.

item.title: HKMA Says It’s Implemented New Bank Rules for Opening Accounts
item.description: Hong Kong has implemented guidelines for banks similar to the new regulatory requirements for brokers on managing mainland Chinese customers’ accounts, the Hong Kong Monetary Authority said.

item.title: Indonesian Officials Vow Again to Stabilize Rupiah, Draw Inflows
item.description: Indonesia’s finance and central bank officials said they will step up efforts to stabilize the currency and attract inflows after a week where the nation’s stocks tumbled at the fastest pace worldwide and its currency sank to all-time lows.

item.title: N.Korea’s Kim Wants Navy as Part of Nuclear War Deterrent: KCNA
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un said the country’s navy must be developed into a force capable of taking charge of part of its nuclear war deterrent plans, KCNA reported Saturday.

item.title: USDA Confirms Second US Screwworm Case in Texas
item.description: A second case of the deadly New World screwworm parasite has been confirmed in a Texas calf, the US Department of Agriculture said.

item.title: US Drops $300 Million Fraud Case That Spotlighted Brian Kahn
item.description: US prosecutors dropped a $300 million fraud indictment against a former executive at Prophecy Asset Management accused of working with Brian Kahn, the disgraced founder of Franchise Group Inc., to cheat investors in their hedge fund.

item.title: Raizen Inks $13 Billion Out-of-Court Debt Deal With Creditors
item.description: Raizen SA reached an out-of-court restructuring agreement with the majority of its creditors, marking a key step in the Brazilian sugar-and-ethanol producer’s efforts to rework its debt, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Colombian Inflation Accelerates to Fastest Pace Since 2024
item.description: Colombian inflation accelerated to its fastest pace since 2024, putting pressure on policymakers to resume interest rate hikes this month.

item.title: Electrum-Backed Mexican Silver Miner Sinda Files for US IPO
item.description: Sinda Ltd. filed for a US initial public offering, seeking to fund mining for silver in a historically productive part of Mexico.

item.title: Tech Selloff, Bitcoin Drop Test Retail Investor Strength Ahead of SpaceX IPO
item.description: For years, Wall Street has benefited from one of the most reliable forces in modern markets: a retail-trader army willing to buy almost anything.

item.title: Marvell Technology, Flex to Join S&P 500 Later This Month
item.description: Marvell Technology Inc. and Flex Ltd. will join the S&P 500 in the latest quarterly rebalance, S&P Dow Jones Indices said Friday.

item.title: Goldman’s Flood Sees Buying Opportunity in Stock Market Selloff
item.description: Friday’s pullback in US equities offers a chance to add exposure rather than a reason to retreat, with a clear path for the S&P 500 to reach 8,000 this year, according to John Flood, the head of Americas equities execution services at Goldman Sachs Group Inc.
